hero.description: The classic Israeli card game where every player plays for themselves. Set your contract, choose the trump, and track every round in a clean digital ledger.

table.title: The Table
table.subtitle: Where Bids Meet Nerve
table.dealerDesc: Bid for the right to choose trump. The highest bid commits you to a minimum number of tricks.
table.fourPlayersDesc: No partners, no safety nets. 13 cards each. Read the room, protect your contract, and play every card with intent.
table.trickTakingDesc: Declare exactly how many tricks you will take. Hit your number, no more, no less.

ledger.title: The Ledger
ledger.subtitle: A Living Scorebook
ledger.description: Every bid, trick, and score is logged. Watch momentum swing as contracts are made or missed.

rules.dealText: All 52 cards are dealt to four players (13 each). The player left of the dealer opens the auction - bid a number of tricks (minimum 5) with a suit, or pass. The highest bid sets trump. If all pass, a Frisch round begins: players exchange 3 cards and bid again with a higher minimum.
rules.playText: After trump is set, each player declares their contract. The total cannot equal 13 - the last bidder must push the game Over or Under. Players must follow suit and may cut with trump when void. The trick winner leads next.
rules.scoreText: Make your contract exactly to score your tricks squared plus ten (e.g., bid 3, take 3 = 19). Miss it and lose points for every trick you are off. A zero bid is high-risk: succeed for a 50-point bonus in Under, fail and take a 50-point penalty.

cta.title: Ready to take your seat?
cta.description: The table is set: four players, one deck, and Israel's sharpest trick-taking game.

tooltips.scoreExplain: Hit your bid exactly: tricks² + 10 points. Miss and lose points per trick off.
tooltips.bidZeroRisk: Bid 0 is high-risk: +50 if you take nothing, -50 if you take any trick.
tooltips.totalBidsBar: Total bids can't equal 13. Last bidder must push Over or Under.
tooltips.trumpExplain: The trump suit beats all other suits. Choose wisely to control the game.
tooltips.noTrumpExplain: No trump means the highest card of the led suit wins each trick.
tooltips.tricksCount: Record exactly how many tricks each player actually took this round.
tooltips.lockBid: Lock your bid so other players can see it. You can't change it after.
tooltips.resetVote: All players must vote yes to reset. Scores will go back to zero.
tooltips.roundScore: This round's score change based on bid vs actual tricks taken.
tooltips.managerBadge: The game manager can edit all players and manage the game.
tooltips.youBadge: This is your seat at the table.
tooltips.overMode: Over: total bids exceed 13. More competition for tricks.
tooltips.underMode: Under: total bids under 13. Someone won't hit their target.
tooltips.scorePositive: Ahead of the pack! Keep hitting those contracts.
tooltips.scoreNegative: Behind on points. A good zero-bid can turn things around.
tooltips.roundHistory: View the complete history of bids, tricks, and scores for every round.
tooltips.deleteGame: Permanently delete this game and all its history. This cannot be undone.

rules.title: Club Rules
rules.subtitle: The complete guide to Israeli Whist
rules.overviewText: Israeli Whist is a trick-taking card game for exactly 4 players. Each player plays for themselves — there are no partnerships. A standard 52-card deck is used, dealing 13 cards to each player. The game revolves around bidding contracts, choosing trump, and taking exactly the number of tricks you declared.
rules.dealText: All 52 cards are dealt evenly to four players (13 each). The player to the left of the dealer opens the trump auction by bidding a number of tricks (minimum 5) with a suit, or passing. Bidding continues clockwise; each new bid must be higher than the previous one. The highest bid sets the trump suit. If all four players pass, a Frisch round begins: players exchange 3 cards with their partner (the player across) and bid again with a higher minimum.
rules.contractsText: After trump is set, each player declares their contract — the exact number of tricks they intend to take. The total of all four contracts cannot equal 13. The last player to declare must adjust their bid to push the total over or under 13. This ensures someone will fail their contract every round.
rules.playText: The player who won the trump auction leads the first trick. Players must follow the led suit if they can. If void in the led suit, they may play trump (cut) or discard. The highest card in the led suit wins the trick, unless trumped — then the highest trump wins. The trick winner leads the next trick.
rules.scoringText: Make your contract exactly: score = tricks² + 10. For example, bid 3 and take exactly 3 = 19 points. Miss your contract: lose 10 points for each trick you are off (over or under). A zero bid is high-risk: if you take zero tricks, you earn +50 in Under mode or +25 in Over mode. If you take any trick after bidding zero, you lose -50 (Under) or -25 (Over).

glossary.trumpDef: The suit that beats all other suits. Determined by the highest bid in the auction.
glossary.contractDef: The number of tricks a player commits to taking in a round. Must be hit exactly for a positive score.
glossary.overDef: When the total bids exceed 13. More competition for tricks — someone will take more than they wanted.
glossary.underDef: When the total bids are below 13. Not enough tricks to go around — someone will fall short.
glossary.frischDef: A re-deal triggered when all four players pass the auction. Players exchange 3 cards and bid again with a higher minimum.
